NASA.FIRMS.Amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 producer endpoint for NASA FIRMS CloudEvents.

NASA.FIRMS.FireDetection.name: FireDetection
NASA.FIRMS.FireDetection.description: A single active-fire / thermal-anomaly pixel detection from a NASA FIRMS satellite product (VIIRS or MODIS), normalised across sensors. Each event is one detection in one satellite overpass; the brightness fields populated depend on `instrument`.

NASA.FIRMS.DataAvailability.name: DataAvailability
NASA.FIRMS.DataAvailability.description: Reference record describing one FIRMS product source: its current data-availability window (min/max date), sensor family, platform, and nominal resolution. Emitted at bridge startup and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret detection freshness and coverage on the same topic as the telemetry.

NASA.FIRMS.mqtt.description: MQTT/5.0 transport variant for NASA FIRMS. Binary-mode CloudEvents routed under geo/fire/firms/<source>/... so consumers can subscribe by source, confidence and coarse geographic tile.

NASA.FIRMS.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 transport variant for NASA FIRMS carrying the same CloudEvents and payload schemas on one broker address.

subject.description: AMQP message subject — stable entity identifier matching the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.
AMQP message subject — stable entity identifier matching the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

FireDetection.name: FireDetection

source.description: FIRMS product source identifier for the satellite/sensor that produced this detection, e.g. `VIIRS_SNPP_NRT`, `VIIRS_NOAA20_NRT`, `VIIRS_NOAA21_NRT`, `MODIS_NRT` (and the `*_SP` standard-processing variants). First segment of the stable identity key `{source}/{record_id}` and the leading MQTT topic level. Populated by the bridge from the requested FIRMS Area API source, not from a CSV column.

record_id.description: Deterministic detection identifier — second segment of the identity key `{source}/{record_id}`. Computed by the bridge as a stable 16-hex-character SHA-1 digest over the immutable detection tuple (source, latitude, longitude, acq_date, acq_time, satellite). Stable across polling cycles so the same fire pixel re-detected in overlapping day-range windows resolves to the same key and is de-duplicated downstream.

latitude.description: Latitude of the centre of the nominal ~375 m (VIIRS) or ~1 km (MODIS) fire pixel, in decimal degrees (WGS84, positive north). Note this is the pixel centroid, not the precise location of the thermal anomaly within the pixel.

longitude.description: Longitude of the centre of the nominal fire pixel, in decimal degrees (WGS84, positive east). Pixel centroid, not the precise anomaly location.

brightness.description: MODIS only: brightness temperature of the fire pixel measured in MODIS channel 21/22 (~4 µm), in Kelvin. Null for VIIRS detections (which report `bright_ti4`/`bright_ti5` instead). Sourced from the MODIS CSV `brightness` column.

bright_t31.description: MODIS only: brightness temperature of the fire pixel measured in MODIS channel 31 (~11 µm), in Kelvin. Used with `brightness` to characterise fire intensity and screen false alarms. Null for VIIRS detections. Sourced from the MODIS CSV `bright_t31` column.

bright_ti4.description: VIIRS only: brightness temperature of the fire pixel measured in VIIRS I-4 channel (~3.74 µm), in Kelvin. Primary fire channel for VIIRS. Null for MODIS detections (which report `brightness` instead). Sourced from the VIIRS CSV `bright_ti4` column.

bright_ti5.description: VIIRS only: brightness temperature of the fire pixel measured in VIIRS I-5 channel (~11.45 µm), in Kelvin. Background/context channel paired with `bright_ti4`. Null for MODIS detections. Sourced from the VIIRS CSV `bright_ti5` column.

scan.description: Along-scan ground dimension of the detected pixel, in kilometres. Grows from nadir toward the swath edge; combined with `track` it gives the actual ground footprint (375 m / 1 km is the nominal nadir resolution only). Sourced from the CSV `scan` column.

track.description: Along-track ground dimension of the detected pixel, in kilometres. Combined with `scan` it gives the actual ground footprint of the detection. Sourced from the CSV `track` column.

acq_date.description: UTC acquisition date of the satellite overpass that produced the detection, formatted `YYYY-MM-DD`. Sourced from the CSV `acq_date` column.

acq_time.description: UTC acquisition time of the satellite overpass, formatted as a zero-padded `HHMM` string (e.g. `0218` = 02:18 UTC). Sourced from the CSV `acq_time` column.

acq_datetime.description: UTC acquisition timestamp combining `acq_date` and `acq_time` into an ISO-8601 instant (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:00Z`). Derived by the bridge.

satellite.description: Short platform code of the acquiring satellite as reported by FIRMS, e.g. `N` (Suomi-NPP), `1` (NOAA-20/JPSS-1), `2` (NOAA-21/JPSS-2) for VIIRS, and `T` (Terra) / `A` (Aqua) for MODIS. Sourced from the CSV `satellite` column.

instrument.description: Sensor that produced the detection: `VIIRS` or `MODIS`. Discriminates which of the brightness fields are populated. Sourced from the CSV `instrument` column.

confidence.description: Raw upstream detection confidence exactly as delivered by FIRMS, kept verbatim for fidelity. For VIIRS this is a class letter `l` (low), `n` (nominal) or `h` (high); for MODIS it is an integer 0–100 (percent) rendered as a string. Use the normalised `confidence_level` for filtering across sensors. Sourced from the CSV `confidence` column.

confidence_level.description: Sensor-normalised detection confidence as a routing-safe class: `low`, `nominal`, or `high`. Derived by the bridge: VIIRS `l`/`n`/`h` map directly; MODIS integer maps <30 → `low`, 30–80 → `nominal`, >80 → `high`. Used as an MQTT topic level so consumers can subscribe by confidence.

version.description: Collection and source-processing version of the detection, e.g. `2.0NRT` (Collection 2, near-real-time) or `2.0` (standard processing). Sourced from the CSV `version` column.

frp.description: Fire Radiative Power in megawatts — the radiative energy release rate of the detected fire within the pixel, a proxy for combustion intensity and a key OSINT discriminator (a multi-hundred-MW spike differs sharply from routine agricultural burning). Sourced from the CSV `frp` column.

daynight.description: Day/Night flag for the overpass: `D` (daytime) or `N` (nighttime), based on solar elevation at the pixel. Night detections are less prone to solar false alarms. Sourced from the CSV `daynight` column.

tile.description: Coarse 10° × 10° geographic tile label of the form `lat<LL>_lon<LL>` (south-west corner, e.g. `lat30_lon-120`) covering the detection. Derived by the bridge purely as an MQTT topic level so consumers can geofence a region of interest without per-message coordinate parsing.

FireDetection.description: A single active-fire / thermal-anomaly pixel detection from a NASA FIRMS satellite product (VIIRS or MODIS), normalised across sensors. Each event is one detection in one satellite overpass; the brightness fields populated depend on `instrument`.

DataAvailability.name: DataAvailability

source.description: FIRMS product source identifier this coverage record describes, e.g. `VIIRS_SNPP_NRT`, `VIIRS_NOAA20_NRT`, `VIIRS_NOAA21_NRT`, `MODIS_NRT`. First segment of the identity key `{source}/{record_id}`; joins to `FireDetection.source`.

record_id.description: Constant literal `coverage` — the second identity-key segment for the single reference record per source (`{source}/coverage`). Keeps the reference family on the same `{source}/{record_id}` shape and topic as telemetry while remaining a stable, idempotent per-source key.

data_id.description: Raw FIRMS data-availability product identifier as returned by the `/api/data_availability` endpoint (the CSV `data_id` column), e.g. `VIIRS_SNPP_NRT`. Usually equal to `source`; preserved verbatim.

min_date.description: Earliest UTC date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) for which this FIRMS source currently has data available via the Area API, from the `min_date` column of the data-availability response.

max_date.description: Most recent UTC date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) for which this FIRMS source currently has data available — the effective freshness horizon of the source, from the `max_date` column.

instrument.description: Sensor family of the source: `VIIRS` or `MODIS`. Derived by the bridge from the source identifier.

satellite.description: Human-readable platform name(s) carried by the source, e.g. `Suomi-NPP`, `NOAA-20`, `NOAA-21`, `Terra`, `Aqua`. Derived by the bridge from the source identifier.

resolution_m.description: Nominal nadir spatial resolution of the source sensor in metres (375 for VIIRS I-band fire product, 1000 for MODIS). Static sensor fact supplied by the bridge for downstream scaling/symbology.

retrieved_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp at which the bridge fetched this availability record.

DataAvailability.description: Reference record describing one FIRMS product source: its current data-availability window (min/max date), sensor family, platform, and nominal resolution. Emitted at bridge startup and refreshed periodically so consumers can interpret detection freshness and coverage on the same topic as the telemetry.
